content.text: FastAPI is a modern Python web framework built on Starlette and Pydantic. It is the default choice for agent API servers: async routes, automatic request/response validation, OpenAPI docs, and dependency injection map cleanly to tool endpoints, streaming LLM proxies, and webhook receivers.
FastAPI exposes HTTP routes as Python functions with type hints. Pydantic models validate JSON bodies and query params; the framework generates OpenAPI schemas and interactive docs at
. Under the hood it runs on ASGI, so
handlers integrate with asyncio, httpx, and streaming responses.

Agent systems expose many HTTP surfaces: chat, tool invocation, health checks, admin hooks. FastAPI reduces boilerplate for validation and documentation while staying async-native — critical when routes await LLM calls lasting seconds.
— typed request/response models catch bad tool payloads early
— non-blocking routes for LLM and HTTP tool calls
— reusable auth, DB sessions, shared httpx clients
— auto-generated schema for frontend and SDK teams
— among the fastest Python frameworks via Starlette/Uvicorn
— deploy with Uvicorn/Hypercorn, not traditional sync WSGI-only stacks
— heavy use of decorators and type hints can obscure flow for newcomers
— blocking SQLAlchemy calls in async routes stall the event loop

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
import httpx

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    app.state.http = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=60.0)
    app.state.agent_pool = AgentPool()
    yield
    await app.state.http.aclose()

app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)

from fastapi import Header, HTTPException, Request

async def verify_api_key(x_api_key: str = Header(...)) -> str:
    if x_api_key not in VALID_KEYS:
        raise HTTPException(401, "invalid api key")
    return x_api_key

async def get_http(request: Request) -> httpx.AsyncClient:
    return request.app.state.http

@app.post("/tools/search")
async def search(
    q: str,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
    client: httpx.AsyncClient = Depends(get_http),
):
    return await search_tool(client, q)
for interactive agent UIs — both are first-class in FastAPI.
for new agent APIs, microservices, async tool gateways, LLM proxies
for minimal sync services or teams already standardized on WSGI
when you need a full admin/ORM monolith alongside agent features

An agent platform exposes POST /v1/runs (start job), GET /v1/runs/{id}/stream (SSE tokens), POST /v1/webhooks/register, and GET /health. Lifespan initializes a shared AsyncClient and Redis pool. Dependencies enforce API keys and rate limits. OpenAPI drives a TypeScript client for the dashboard.
— use async drivers or asyncio.to_thread for sync libraries
— leaks internal fields; define explicit output models
— use Uvicorn with async workers for concurrent agent sessions
Structure routers by domain: agent.py, tools.py, webhooks.py
Use Pydantic models for every public request and response shape
Configure lifespan for shared clients and graceful shutdown
Set timeouts on upstream LLM HTTP calls via shared AsyncClient
Version APIs with prefix /v1 and keep OpenAPI stable
SQLModel or async SQLAlchemy for persistence
Prometheus middleware for request latency on agent routes
FastAPI is a typed reception desk: every visitor (HTTP request) is checked against a schema before reaching your agent logic — invalid payloads never enter the building.
FastAPI = async ASGI framework with Pydantic validation and OpenAPI
Default for agent API servers: lifespan, Depends, StreamingResponse
Never block the event loop in async routes
Reuse HTTP clients and define explicit response models
Deploy with Uvicorn; organize routes with APIRouter
